MELItem.Title: Flap/Slat Hydraulic Control Module (HCM) Power Control

ListItem.Para: The associated hydraulic control module channel is deactivated.
Slat secondary mode is verified to operate normally once each flight day.
Flap secondary mode is verified to operate normally once each flight day.

Note.Para: The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 27-51-05.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate associated HCM channel, verify operation of slat secondary mode once each flight day, and verify flap secondary mode operates normally once each flight day (AMM DDG 27-51-06).

ListItem.Para: Deactivate associated HCM channel.
Confirm flaps are UP.
Remove power to C1 and R Flight Control Electronics (FCE) by opening circuit breakers FCE CABINET- C1 BACKUP and FCE CABINET- R BACKUP and locking with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Install landing gear downlock pins.
Gain access to flap/slat HCM in the right main landing gear wheel well.
Disconnect, cap and stow associated HCM A or B electrical connector.

Note.Para: The FLAP/SLAT SOLENOID COIL status message will be displayed. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 27-51-05

ListItem.Para: Close circuit breakers FCE CABINET- C1 BACKUP and FCE CABINET- R BACKUP.
Verify remaining HCM channel operates normally.
For all three hydraulic systems available, turn hydraulics on.
For only one or two of the hydraulic systems available at once:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever UP.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right.
Pressurize the center hydraulic system.
Confirm that all spoilers are extended.
Using the flap lever, select flaps 5.
On the EICAS flap display, confirm the indication reaches the 5 position and that the 5 indication turns green.
Using the flap lever, select flaps UP.
On the EICAS flap display, confirm the indication reaches the UP position and that the UP indication turns green.
Confirm the status message for the remaining HCM channel is not displayed.
Confirm the FLAP/SLAT SOLENOID COIL status message is displayed.
Depressurize the center hydraulic system.
Remove landing gear downlock pins.
Do the ground test of the slat secondary mode.
Confirm flaps are UP.
Conduct the ground system test Slats (Electric).
On the expanded flap and slat position indications, confirm leading edge extension to the mid position, then retraction to UP.
After completion of the test, confirm the SLATS SECONDARY FAIL status message is not displayed.
For trailing edge variable camber (cruise flaps) function operating normally, confirm status message FLAPS SECONDARY FAIL is not displayed.
For trailing edge variable camber (cruise flaps) function inoperative or disabled:
Do the ground test of the flap secondary mode.
Confirm flaps are UP.
For all three hydraulic systems available, turn hydraulics on.
For only one or two of the hydraulic systems available at once:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever UP.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are extended.
Conduct the ground system test Flaps (Electric).

Note.Para: If hydraulic systems are pressurized, spoilers, ailerons, and flaperons will move during test.

ListItem.Para: After completion of the test, confirm the FLAPS SECONDARY FAIL status message is not displayed.
Retract spoilers if extended:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever DN.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are retracted.
